spatial_join.description: Join attributes from one vector layer to another based on spatial relationship. Uses spatial indexing for efficient processing.

input_vector.label: Target vector layer. Features from this layer will receive joined attributes.

join_vector.label: Source vector layer. Attributes from this layer will be joined to the target features.

strategy.label: Aggregation strategy {first, last, count, sum, mean, min, max} (default first). Controls how multiple matches are combined: 'first' takes first match, 'count' tallies matches, 'sum' accumulates values, 'mean' averages them.
strategy.tooltip: Use 'count' to count overlapping features, 'mean' to average attributes, 'sum' for totals. Strategy only matters when multiple source features match one target feature.

predicate.label: Spatial relationship {intersects, contains, within, touches} (default intersects). Choose based on your workflow needs.
predicate.tooltip: intersects: any overlap; contains: target fully contains source; within: target fully inside source; touches: share boundary only

near.description: Find nearest neighbor features and optionally compute distance. Efficient for proximity analysis and distance calculations.

input_vector.label: Input vector layer. Nearest neighbors will be found for each feature in this layer.

search_vector.label: Search layer. Nearest features in this layer will be identified (can be same as input).

find_all.label: Find all equidistant neighbors [optional]
find_all.tooltip: When enabled, returns all features at the same minimum distance (default: only first match). Useful when multiple features are equally close.

max_distance.label: Maximum search distance in layer units [optional]
max_distance.tooltip: Limits search radius to improve performance on large datasets. Leave empty for unlimited search.

buffer.description: Expand or contract features by a specified distance, creating buffer zones. Rounded for polygons; variable offset possible with attribute field.

distance.label: Buffer distance in layer units (can be negative to shrink). Positive values expand features outward; negative values contract them inward.
distance.tooltip: For polygons: expand outward (positive) or inward (negative). For lines: creates band around line. Distance should match layer units (meters, feet, degrees, etc.).

cap_style.label: End cap style {flat, round, square} (default round). Applies only to line buffers.
cap_style.tooltip: round: smooth caps (most common); square: square caps; flat: no cap extension

convex_hull.description: Compute convex hull (minimum bounding geometry) for features. Useful for outlining feature groups or simplifying geometries.

input_vector.label: Vector layer. Convex hull will be computed for all features combined or per-group if grouping is enabled.

group.label: Group by attribute [optional]
group.tooltip: If specified, computes separate convex hull for each unique attribute value. Useful for computing bounds per category.

dissolve.description: Merge adjacent or overlapping features with same attribute value. Combines geometries and can aggregate numeric attributes.

group_field.label: Group by attribute. Features with same value in this field are merged into one feature.
group_field.tooltip: Leave empty to merge all features into a single feature. Choose attribute with distinct categories (e.g., 'region', 'zone').

aggregate_mode.label: Aggregation for numeric fields {first, sum, mean, min, max, count} (default first). Controls how non-group attributes are combined.
aggregate_mode.tooltip: first: keep first value; sum: total; mean: average; count: tally of merged features

simplify_features.description: Simplify geometries by removing detail while preserving shape. Reduces complexity for visualization or processing speed.

tolerance.label: Simplification tolerance in layer units (default 1.0). Higher values remove more detail.
tolerance.tooltip: Controls how much simplification occurs. Typical range: 0.1–10.0 depending on coordinate scale. Test values to find balance between speed and detail preservation.

preserve_topology.label: Preserve topology [optional]
preserve_topology.tooltip: When enabled, prevents self-intersections during simplification. Recommended for polygons to avoid invalid geometries.

densify_features.description: Add intermediate vertices to geometries at regular intervals. Improves accuracy for curved features or when reprojecting.

interval.label: Vertex spacing in layer units (default 1.0). New vertices inserted at this interval along each segment.
interval.tooltip: Smaller intervals add more vertices (more detail, larger file). Typical range: 0.1–10.0 depending on feature complexity and coordinate scale.

points_along_line.description: Generate points at regular intervals along lines. Useful for sampling, measurement, or creating regular point grids along features.

input_vector.label: Line layer. Points will be generated along each line.

interval.label: Point spacing in layer units (default 1.0). Points placed at this distance along lines.
interval.tooltip: Smaller intervals create denser point coverage. Actual positions may vary slightly from interval if line length is not exact multiple of interval.

endpoint.label: Include endpoint [optional]
endpoint.tooltip: When enabled, ensures final point placed at line endpoint even if interval doesn't align exactly.

clip_vector.description: Clip vector features to a boundary. Only features or parts within the clip boundary are retained.

clip_vector.label: Clip boundary layer. Features will be cut to this boundary.

keep_interior.label: Keep interior holes [optional]
keep_interior.tooltip: For polygon clips with holes: when enabled, preserves holes from clip boundary; when disabled, fills them. Rarely needed.
